Tylenol Sinus Congestion and Pain Non- Drowsy Rapid Release Gels are one of dozens of over the counter health aids from Tylenol, a company with products that seem to work wonders for some people while having almost no effect on others. This particular product falls into the latter category for me, with barely noticeable changes in sinus congestion, even after taking a couple of tablets.

On the good side, these tablets are easy to take thanks to the gelcaps that, while a little larger than average, slide down easily and effortlessly. The manufacturer recommends taking one or two of these caplets every four hours and it claims that this product can provide temporary relief from headache, sinus congestion, sinus pressure, nasal congestion, and minor body aches and pains. Based on my experience, the relief is only slight. This product is so- so at relieving pain, and it deserves at least an average rating in this area, but the main reason I take a medicine like this is to get relief from sinus congestion and pressure. This Tylenol product does almost nothing. I expect at least a little loosening of the congestion, but there is practically no noticeable difference at all.

A large part of the reason this Tylenol product is ineffective at its intended purpose is that they are made using phenylephrine. This is a newer decongestant substance and while it might work well for some people, it has almost no effect on my sinus problems. However, I have noticed that other sinus relief products, containing the same ingredient, work a little better. So, the problem with Tylenol Sinus Congestion Rapid Release Gels must have something to do with the other ingredients, even though the phenylephrine is the main issue.

Sinus problems are irritating, but Tylenol Sinus Congestion and Pain Non- Drowsy Rapid Release Gels do little to eliminate the problem. They perform at an acceptable level in the pain relief category, but their inability to reduce sinus congestion is inexcusable and when you add to that the fact that a dose is good for only four hours, you have a product that cannot be recommended.

My son is 21. 5’2” and about 119 pounds. He is small for his age. His bladder is not. He has just in the last year outgrown the GoodNites (children’s) pull-on disposable ‘underwear’. I can assure you that the children’s underwear held more fluid than these adult small do. I have to strip him, his bedding, his covers, wash EVERYTHING every single morning. In the year since he outgrew the GoodNites we’ve used both the Depends brand and the Assurance brand. These are about on par with the -much, much cheaper- Assurance, in terms of what amount of fluid they hold: Not much. I am a stickler for changing him as frequently as I am aware he has wet...we have zero skin breakdown thus far and I want to keep it that way. Another thing is when he outgrew the GoodNites, he takes a small in both the Depends and Assurance brands. I ordered a small in these after double checking the size chart. I ordered 80, opened all the packages, put them in the drawer. First night I get one out and they almost do not fit. I am using these very expensive, crappy (no pun intended) ‘underwear’ round the clock and changing them every few hours, even when dry because if he gains a single pound they won’t fit. The Depends are what I’m going back to after these run out. I am BEYOND disappointed!

My husband has severe rheumatoid arthritis and his hands are seriously deformed with Heberdin Nodes (nasty lg. "lumps" that twist his fingers). He does need to have his finger joints replaced, but has been trying to delay the surgery for as long as possible.  I want you to understand that his condition is really bad, so maybe medications are not going to much help.  I had encouraged him to keep taking the pills and give them time to work. He's been using them for the time recommended to see improvement.  He has indicated that he has experienced some relief and perhaps a little more mobility, however, not enough to justify taking them.  It looks like he's just going to have to get the surgery. If you are in earlier stages, perhaps this would work, intact based on my husbands experience I'm sure the capsules would provide relief, but if you have fingers that are twisted up its surgery or nothing.

The photo shown, the product description, "Thick-It 2" is what you believe you're getting. So when you get what is labeled as "THICK IT ORIGINAL" you'll think you're getting the regular Thick-It. It's not, if it is in the blue lidded with white and blue labeled container. Somehow, dropping the "2" and adding the word "Original" above smaller, less noticeable lettering that says "Concentrate" seemed like a good idea to the manufacturers. So identical almost to the actual "Original Thick-It", except for the actual "Original Thick-It" is in a yellow lidded container with a white and blue label that also says "Original".

If you're like me and have only ever used Thick-It2, never have you ever bought the actual "Original Thick-It" (less concentrated), therefore had no idea that the sole difference now would be in the dropping of the number "2" and the color coding of the lid, you might get your package and think you got the Original Original. Yep... it's THAT confusing. I had to call the manufacturer. The employee who answered the phone got so confused they had to put me on hold to clarify with someone else. I'm not exaggerating.

All of the confusion aside, I also asked (when I phoned the manufacturer) if the formula had changed. They responded with a VERY QUICK, VERY ABSOLUTE "No". Couldn't recall which formula came in which color lid, but knew resolutely that the formula hadn't changed. Mmkay. All I know is I could thicken my kid's 2 cups into pudding consistency with 2-3 tablespoons of Thick-It2 when I last bought it several years ago and now it takes 2/3 - 3/4 cup of the supposedly unchanged product. (His swallow issues are solely with liquids [and are both physical and psychological]; he's stayed fairly hydrated for several years but he's had a flare up [of the psychological aspect] recently and I've needed to start adding über thickened fluids back into his diet to keep him hydrated.)

My star rating doesn't deduct anything for the confusing labeling issues. I just detailed all of that for any other consumer out there who, like me, has never bought the Original Original and might not realize they're getting the same product as they ordered. My star rating is because, despite the manufacturer's claim they haven't changed the formula for the Concentrated Thick-It2, it is not as thickening as it was 10 years ago. BUT it is still better than the other brand that some hospitals use when you need to thicken water/fluids into pudding consistency.
I've tried several protein powder/meal replacements through the years, and this one is my go-to product.

I’ve submitted a review for the other brand I used first; you will have to click my user name and look for my other reviews to find it, as I believe cross-referencing brands in reviews is at best redacted by Amazon.

That said I found this brand, decent reviews, larger quantity, better price, thought I’d try it. And the weirdest result has occurred: The oil is orange, it is heavy in weight like oil (not light in weight like water), and initially smells divine in my diffusers (I have 3 large 450 ml capacity and a small 150 ml capacity...yes, my home smells like an explosion in an orange orchard. I like that!! Lol). The weird problem is that after very little time in my diffuser, the oily water becomes cloudy white water and the diffusers seize working. I empty them, I wash them well, I dry them, I run a cycle of just water through...and the diffusers work as new. I have light beige carpet.  Some idiot (I mean me) got 4 puppies over 3 months; as soon as 1 puppy was housebroken, I got a new puppy and the first one would have to be housebroken all over again.  Needless to say, my carpet on the landing of my stairs looked like 20 year old rental carpet (pee stain-wise).  I tried EVERYTHING in my carpet cleaner (it's a Kent, which is like a Rug Doctor) and nothing worked so I decided "That's it!  I'm tearing it out!" - BUT before I tore it out, I decided to experiment.  My experiment was to see what 3 cups Clorox bleach to 3 gallons of water would do to my carpet:  If it ruined it, well, I was ripping it out anyway.  Low 'n behold, it worked!  My carpet looked great AND it wasn't splotchy or overly bleached.  Fast forward and I have a litter of 4 puppies and they had 3 accidents on my landing.  These pups are less than 3# each, so the wee stains were less than 3 inches each.  Instead of toxic cleaning the carpet again, I got the BISSELL OXYPRO PET and followed the directions to the inth degree.  Now, I have 3 huge (8-10" each) dark spots on the carpet.  I used 2 bottles of the Bissell over time, thinking I wasn't using enough to actually soak the stains (that's how the spots got so big).  Each time, I used the Bissell after bedtime and then placed a box fan on the area overnight so that it would be completely dry before foot-traffic hit it in the morning.  Fortunately, I found this stuff called GET SERIOUS and it got the Bissell stains out.  *****I should note that I got my Bissell as an 'included' when I purchased my Bissell Trilogy Pet vacuum (which I LOVE!).
I have been using this for about a week, 1x a day. I can already notice a difference in my nails! Anxious to see what other benefits will come after longer, continuous use.
